text.title: employer-branding Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for employer-branding as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Auto-publishing Glassdoor responses without human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated responses that contradict employee experience or sound legalistic create PR incidents that are harder to recover from than the original review.

failure-mode.description: Repurposing one testimonial format across all channels without adaptation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 300-word LinkedIn testimonial becomes unreadable on Instagram; a 30-second video quote loses context in a blog post. Repurpose with channel-specific editing, not copy-paste.

failure-mode.description: Cliché injection in every brand draft: "passionate team," "world-class culture," "amazing people."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These phrases appear on 90%+ of employer brand pages and provide zero differentiation. They train candidates to ignore the content.

failure-mode.description: Generating fake employee names or composite quotes when raw material is missing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fabricated employee voices are unethical and constitute false advertising in candidate-facing materials. Always require real employee input before drafting.

failure-mode.description: Brand voice drift across 50+ content generations without re-anchoring.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents drift from the established voice even when the brand guide is in context. Include 3 approved sample posts in every content generation prompt to re-anchor tone.

failure-mode.description: Hashtag stuffing: LLMs add 15+ hashtags per post.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LinkedIn best practice is 3–5 per post; overstuffing signals low-quality content and suppresses reach.

failure-mode.description: Image rights violations: agents pull stock images that may be unlicensed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use whitelisted libraries only (Unsplash, company DAM) to avoid legal exposure.

failure-mode.description: Localization mishap: a US-positive phrase ("crushing it") translates poorly across markets.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must localize per market, not direct-translate; market-specific language is the agent's responsibility before submission.
